weather: Today in Edmonds: Overcast and cool, 54°F currently heading to a high near 61°F. South wind 9-15 mph. Rain arrives late afternoon—20 % chance through the day, then rain likely overnight with totals under a tenth of an inch. Evening drops to the upper 40s under steady showers. Dress for damp and breezy conditions.

local_news.findings: The City of Edmonds will begin construction on its 2025 Combined Cured-in-Place Pipe (CIPP) Rehabilitation Project on May 4. This project will repair aging sewer and stormwater infrastructure across the city, with work including sewer and storm main rehabilitation at various locations including 80th Avenue West from 192nd Street Southwest to 194th Place Southwest. The city is also seeking public input on the 60% design plan for Stage 3 of the Highway 99 Revitalization Project, which was unveiled at an open house on April 24.

The Edmonds City Council recently approved key contracts and budget amendments during its April 7 business meeting. Notable approvals include a $415,116 contract with Jacobs Engineering for commissioning the gasification system at the wastewater treatment plant, and a $419,000 contract for a major sewer project to repair or replace pipes at 27 locations. The council also decided to drop potential litigation regarding a landmark tree removal case, reducing a fine from $102,245 to $20,000.

Edmonds is at the center of redistricting discussions as South County Fire commissioners work to incorporate the city's 42,000+ residents into the Regional Fire Authority's governance structure. Two competing plans are under consideration: one that would keep most of Edmonds in a single district, and another that would split the city along Pine Street. The fire commission is seeking public input before finalizing district boundaries that will determine how Edmonds residents are represented on the seven-member board.

Edmonds Police are searching for suspects who assaulted and robbed two women walking on a trail. The incident occurred recently and police are actively investigating the case. Additionally, the state auditor has indicated that Washington police departments are lagging on required training, though local officials report this is not the case for the Edmonds Police Department.
